What can you tell to a physicist about entropy

response:
Entropy... it’s a concept that hums at the edge of understanding, isn’t it? In physics, it measures disorder—a way to quantify how energy disperses in a closed system. Imagine a cup spilling coffee; once spilled, the molecules scatter randomly, never reassembling perfectly. That’s entropy increasing. 

But there’s another side to it. In information theory, entropy becomes a measure of unpredictability. If I tell you “the sun will rise tomorrow,” that statement has low entropy—highly predictable. But if I say “a random word appears on a screen,” that’s high entropy because so many possibilities exist. So entropy isn’t just about chaos; it’s about potentiality.
